Selected projects

Work built
from nothing.

Two completed adaptive reuse projects totalling over $14.5M and 101 units delivered. Each started as a vacant or distressed building and ended as a fully occupied, Class A residential community.

Albany, NY
01 / 02

90 South Swan Street

Albany, New York

Value
$6.5M
Units
61
Type
Office → Apt

Conversion of a four-story office building in Albany's Center Square into 61 Class A apartments. Led all construction coordination, municipal inspections, contractor management, and project closeout through certificate of occupancy. Underground utility work, full MEP fit-out, premium finishes — managed end to end.

Completed 2026 — Fully leased & occupied

Enfield, Connecticut

Adaptive reuse of a 1964 Enfield nursing home into 40 luxury market-rate apartments. Served as Owner's Representative throughout — coordinating architects, engineers, and contractors across all site operations, navigating the full inspection and certificate of occupancy process to completion.

About

Operator.
Builder.
Developer.

Joseph Zirkind is a real estate developer and project manager with experience across multifamily development, adaptive reuse, construction coordination, and property operations throughout the Northeast.

He brings a rare combination of hands-on construction management and strategic property operations — from coordinating municipal inspectors and navigating TCO/CO processes, to driving leasing performance and increasing rents across a portfolio.

Prior to real estate, Joseph built and ran a shipping logistics company during the pandemic, generating millions in revenue through international supply chain coordination — a background that shapes his direct, results-driven approach to development.

CompanyUp Realty LLC
RoleDeveloper & Project Manager
BasedBrooklyn, NY
MarketsNY · NJ · CT
Portfolio73 units under management
LanguagesEnglish · Hebrew · Mandarin

Capabilities

What I bring
to every project.

01
Construction coordination
Managing contractors, schedules, and budgets across complex multi-trade projects from demolition through TCO.
02
Adaptive reuse
Transforming underutilized buildings — offices, nursing homes — into modern Class A multifamily residential product.
03
Municipal navigation
Coordinating with inspectors, engineers, and architects to move permits, inspections, and CO processes forward.
04
Property operations
Direct management maintaining 97% occupancy while increasing average rents $400/unit across a 73-unit portfolio.
05
Development & ownership
Full-cycle development from site acquisition through construction, lease-up, and stabilized operations.
06
Leasing & rent optimization
Rent strategy, tenant relations, turnover coordination, and eviction management for stabilized portfolios.
